title: Novel ‘Le Sang du Pouvoir’ by Hubert Kolani delves into African power
content:
‘In African politics, power isn’t granted; it’s seized.’

Originally published on Global Voices

Photo by Hubert Kolani. Used with permission.

In spaces where freedom of expression is under threat, criticism seldom leads to meaningful change. As journalists and essayists face significant risks in addressing the inner workings of power, fiction offers an alternative solution.

This is the gamble Hubert Kolani, a trained lawyer and young Togolese author, took with his first novel, “Le Sang du Pouvoir” (The Blood of Power). In this French-language novel, he candidly explores the concerning inner workings of African politics, which he calls “dar
snippet: ‘In African politics, power isn’t granted; it’s seized.’Originally published on Global VoicesPhoto by Hubert Kolani. Used with permission. In spaces where freedom of expression is under threat, critic
creator: Laura
sourceDescription: Citizen media stories from around the world

title: Young men, masculinity and states of emergency in the Caribbean
content:
Under state of emergency regulations, search powers expand and detention without charge becomes permissible

Originally published on Global Voices

Feature image via Canva Pro.
